title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the typed callback and context registry contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed callback and context registry after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Function Pointer Compatibility · changed case · Review Function Pointer Compatibility
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 2219. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
